notes: Official rates from City of Milwaukee Comptroller's 2025 Combined Property Tax Report (Bill Christianson, CPFO). Assessment year 2025, funding 2026 budgets. Gross rate excludes Lottery & First Dollar Tax Credits. School Levy Tax Credit shown separately.
source: City of Milwaukee Comptroller, 2025 Combined Property Tax Report (TaxBrochure2025.pdf)

additionalCredits.note: Per-parcel flat amounts, not per-$1,000 rates. Applied to qualifying residential properties.

jurisdictions.name: Milwaukee Public Schools
jurisdictions.source: City of Milwaukee Comptroller, 2025 Combined Property Tax Report
jurisdictions.verificationNotes: Official Comptroller rate of $9.66/1K. Levy of $427.1M (3.0% increase from $414.8M). MPS receives 43 cents of every property tax dollar. Rate decreased due to rising assessed values despite higher levy. Excludes school levy tax credit of $1.62/1K.
jurisdictions.desc: K-12 education for ~66,000 students across 130+ schools
jurisdictions.detail: Largest share of your tax bill. Funds teacher salaries, school operations, special education, transportation, building maintenance, and extracurricular programs.
jurisdictions.fundingNote: MPS levy grew significantly due to voter-approved April 2024 referendum. Rate dropped from $10.61 to $9.66 as assessed values rose faster than the levy.

jurisdictions.name: City of Milwaukee
jurisdictions.source: City of Milwaukee Comptroller, 2025 Combined Property Tax Report
jurisdictions.verificationNotes: Official Comptroller rate of $7.61/1K. Levy of $336.8M (3.9% increase). City receives 34 cents of every property tax dollar. Note: Urban Milwaukee reported $7.64 at Council adoption — the $0.03 difference is due to final assessed value adjustments.
jurisdictions.desc: Police, fire, public works, libraries, health, and all city services
jurisdictions.detail: Funds city government operations across 21+ departments including police, fire, DPW, libraries, health, neighborhood services, and administration.

jurisdictions.name: Milwaukee County
jurisdictions.source: City of Milwaukee Comptroller, 2025 Combined Property Tax Report
jurisdictions.verificationNotes: Official Comptroller rate of $3.15/1K. Levy of $139.2M (2.9% increase). County receives 14 cents of every property tax dollar.
jurisdictions.desc: Parks, transit, courts, behavioral health, and county services
jurisdictions.detail: Funds the county park system (15,000+ acres), Milwaukee County Transit System, county courts, medical examiner, and human services programs.

jurisdictions.name: Milwaukee Metropolitan Sewerage District
jurisdictions.source: City of Milwaukee Comptroller, 2025 Combined Property Tax Report
jurisdictions.verificationNotes: Official Comptroller rate of $1.24/1K. Levy of $54.6M (1.7% increase). MMSD receives 6 cents of every property tax dollar.
jurisdictions.desc: Wastewater treatment, flood management, and water quality
jurisdictions.detail: Operates the Jones Island and South Shore water reclamation facilities, manages the deep tunnel system, and funds overflow reduction and flood management projects.

jurisdictions.name: Milwaukee Area Technical College
jurisdictions.source: City of Milwaukee Comptroller, 2025 Combined Property Tax Report
jurisdictions.verificationNotes: Official Comptroller rate of $0.76/1K. Levy of $33.8M (-2.6% decrease). MATC receives 3 cents of every property tax dollar.
jurisdictions.desc: Workforce training, associate degrees, and continuing education
jurisdictions.detail: Serves 30,000+ students at 4 campuses offering 170+ programs in healthcare, IT, manufacturing, and more.

medianHomeExample.note: Gross rate of $22.42/1K (sum of 5 jurisdictions) applied. School levy credit of $1.62/1K deducted from MPS portion yields net rate of $20.80/1K. Lottery credit ($260.35) and First Dollar Credit ($86.78) not included as they vary by property.
